steps.title: Spot what needs identifying
steps.content: The sentence claims “there was one personality trait” and then must reveal which trait. The phrase “openness to experience” provides that identification—it's the answer to the implied question “which trait?”.

steps.title: Choose punctuation that introduces the identification
steps.content:
A colon appropriately introduces “openness to experience” as the clarification of which trait the sentence refers to. Choice D (high on:) signals that what follows identifies the trait.

Choice A creates a fragment (“On openness to experience” as a dangling phrase). Choice B incorrectly uses a semicolon to join an independent clause with a noun phrase. Choice C omits punctuation that conventionally marks the shift from describing the trait to naming it.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
